commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Then did I beat them as fine as the dust before the wind: I did cast them out as the dirt in the streets. Augustine of Hippo 43. "And I will beat them as small as dust before the face of the wind" (ver. And I will beat them small; for dry they are, receiving not the shower of God's mercy; that borne aloft and puffed up with pride they may be hurried along from firm and unshaken hope, and as it were from the earth's solidity and stability. "As the clay of the streets I will destroy them. " In their wanton and loose course along the broad ways of perdition, which many walk, will I destroy them.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Streets. Thus he treated the Ammonites, 2 Kings viii. 2., and xii. 31. (Calmet)
Jesus Christ will rule over his enemies with a rod of iron. (Berthier)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
